- Repository: `forever-treasure-companion` (`Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`)
- Original branch/commit: `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild` at `fae0ad1` (2026-07-17 19:59 -04:00)

- No local-only commits: the original HEAD is an ancestor of the selected GitHub baseline.
- Preserved:
  - Backup branch: `backup/work-computer-pre-sync-20260720-141933`
  - Stash: `stash@{0}` — contains `node_modules.pnpm-failed/@prisma/client/runtime/query_engine_bg.sqlserver.wasm-base64.js`
- Existing local branches and the sibling Phase 3 worktree were retained.

## GitHub state

- Default branch: `origin/main` at `746118b` — `docs: record Phase 3 finalization provenance`
- Selected authoritative implementation branch: `origin/codex/project-lanternwake-phase-4-bring-the-harbor-alive`
- Selected commit: `ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04`
  - Date: 2026-07-19 19:01 -04:00
  - Message: `feat: complete Project Lanternwake Phase 4`

It was selected because it contains the Phase 2/3 implementation lineage plus the two Phase 4 commits, Phase 4 governing records, tests, and the harbor/library/invitation/waiting-room/Studio source changes. `origin/main` has four divergent Phase 3 finalization/provenance commits but no Phase 4 implementation. No merge was performed.

The first fetch completed ref transfer but its optional UNC pack-maintenance step hit a permission error; a maintenance-disabled repeat fetch succeeded, and `git fsck` passed.

## Local synchronized baseline

- Current branch: `work/lanternwake-latest`
- Upstream: `origin/codex/project-lanternwake-phase-4-bring-the-harbor-alive`
- Local HEAD and remote HEAD: `ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04`
- Ahead/behind: `0 / 0`
- Worktree: clean
- No merge commit or stale-file carryover was created.
- `Codex_Chats/` and `Development_Docs/` have no diff from the selected GitHub commit.

## Lanternwake status

- Latest strictly fully validated phase: Phase 1, *Light the Lantern*.
- Phase 2, *Claim the Deck*, has accepted implementation/composite validation, with a historical environment issue in its combined gate.
- Phase 3, *Unfurl the Tale*, has source implementation but lacks its own final integrated verdict.
- Phase 4, *Bring the Harbor Alive*, is implementation-complete and focused-validated, but not formally accepted:
  - 241 requirements are focused-pass (119 MX + 122 OA).
  - Focused evidence includes 38 suites / 237 tests, 2 Phase 4 browser tests, 23 offline-recovery tests, 33 snapshot tests, typecheck, and manifest validation.
  - The required serialized `npm run validate`, database-isolation proof, and final chat/document validation remain pending.
- Phase 5, *Awaken the Relics*, has not begun; authored Rive/Lottie/PageFlip work remains the handoff.
- Phase 6 has not begun.

The newest relevant archived chat is `Implement Lanternwake Phase 2`, updated 2026-07-19 02:23Z. There is no archived conversation mentioning `7521afa` or `ec19f21`; the archive is Git-identical to the selected remote branch but is stale as Phase 4 evidence.

## Preserved material

- Backup branch and stash remain intact.
- No local branches, commits, staged changes, untracked files, development documents, or archived chats were deleted.
- No push, merge, rebase, reset, clean, or source implementation occurred.
- I stopped one confirmed stale orphaned `next start` process on port 3001; no files were affected.

## Environment status

- Repository package manager: npm (`npm@11.9.0`); lockfile present.
- `package.json` and `package-lock.json` changed between the original checkout and baseline, so deterministic `npm ci` is needed.
- The refresh could not run because `npm` is absent from this shell and bundled Node has no npm executable. No dependency or lockfile mutation occurred.
- Canonical server: `http://localhost:3000` is running from the launcher-managed local mirror.
- Ports 3101 and 3106 are clear; the stale 3001 listener was removed.
- No tests or server restart were run, per synchronization-only scope.

Parallel review used four read-only lanes (governing records, archive, source/environment, and Git provenance), with three concurrent at most; all findings were reviewed and reconciled.

The work-computer repository is now synchronized to commit ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04, which is the new verified baseline. No new implementation work has been started.

Cause: network workspace/runtime state. Fix: use the repository local runtime mirror and clean validation workflow. [Task 1]\n90-- Symptom: media E2E failures or ambiguous catalog assertions. Cause: signature-only image fixtures or unscoped selectors with multiple published tales. Fix: generate decoder-readable PNGs with Sharp and scope selectors to the target card/component. [Task 1]\n91-- Symptom: UNC Git reports geometric-repack permission warning after commit. Fix: treat it separately from publication success; verify commit SHA, upstream parity, and clean status. [Task 2]\n92-\n93-# Task Group: Forever Treasure Companion project archive sync\n94:scope: Rendered/redacted project conversation archival, manifest validation, and conservative Git finalization for the companion repo; distinct from raw native-session backup.\n95-applies_to: cwd=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion; reuse_rule=safe for the same `Codex_Chats` layout; re-check accessible sources and branch state each run.\n96-\n--\n128-- The parent workspace is not the Git repo; use `git -C <candidate> status --short --branch`. Local Codex JSONL under `~/.codex/sessions` plus `session_index.jsonl` are the durable sources; live ChatGPT Project conversations were not directly accessible. [Task 1]\n129-- `scripts/sync_codex_chats.py` ingests local JSONL and supplied ChatGPT exports, redacts secrets, renders YAML-front-matter Markdown, maintains `Codex_Chats/manifest.json`, validates hashes, and uses atomic writes/locking. `.codex/chat-sync.json`, `Codex_Chats/README.md`, and `AGENTS.md` define behavior. [Task 2]\n130:- Use `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \".\\scripts\\sync_codex_chats.ps1\" -DryRun` when direct `.ps1` execution is policy-blocked. The key proof is a final write/validation followed by an identical no-op rerun; re-sync near finalization because the active transcript can grow. [Task 2]\n131-\n132-## Failures and how to do differently\n133-\n134-- Symptom: no full transcript through thread APIs. Cause: summarized/shape-limited surfaces. Fix: use raw JSONL and session index; do not imply access to unavailable project chats. [Task 1]\n135:- Symptom: secret detector blocks fixture or synchronizer text, or manifest hash fails. Fix: require structurally valid JWTs before blocking, exclude synchronizer self-calls, and keep front-matter/body blank-separator normalization identical in rendering and validation. [Task 2]\n136-\n137-# Task Group: treasurehunt Phase 3 command center and player refresh\n--\n167-## Reusable knowledge\n168-\n169:- The UNC worktree is mirrored by the launcher to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development`; this local mirror is the reliable dev/validation runtime. `scripts/test-all.ps1` is the end-to-end gate. [Task 1]\n170-- Reconcile additively: Phase 2 owns player rendering/visibility and seed presets; Phase 3 layers administrative models/API/UI (`PreparedAction`, `CommandExecution`, `PlayerPresence`, Quartermaster) on top. Use a normal merge, not rebase/force-push. [Task 1]\n171-- The refresh bug was split persistence: persist `forever-journal-open:<campaign>` in `sessionStorage`, restore only after hydration, then restart the launcher. Live Playwright verification expected `openJournalButtons: 0`, `titleOverlays: 0`, `workspaceHidden: \"false\"`. [Task 2]\n--\n177-\n178-# Task Group: Forever Treasure Companion foundation and Phase 2 player shell\n179:scope: Standalone Next.js/Prisma companion foundation, safe public-repo player shell, validation, and GitHub handoff.\n180-applies_to: cwd=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion; reuse_rule=safe for this application family; re-check public/private repository status before adding real story material.\n181-\n--\n207-## Reusable knowledge\n208-\n209:- The companion is a standalone Next.js App Router + Prisma application with SSE progression, transactional state, player/GM flows, scripts/docs, and clean-clone validation. [Task 1]\n210:- The Player Companion server surface is projection-based: emit allowlisted safe data to players and keep raw/unreleased payloads hidden. `scripts/test-all.ps1` validates format/lint/typecheck/unit/browser/database/build/restart; a local temp clone is the reliable validation pivot for UNC pnpm/Playwright/Prisma runs. [Task 2]\n211-- Public repository status is a hard story-safety boundary. `docs/known-limitations.md` and `docs/codex-handoff.md` are sources of truth for phase status and validated handoff SHA. [Task 2]\n212-\n213-## Failures and how to do differently\n214-\n215:- Symptom: validation collides with stale `.next`, Playwright artifacts, or long-lived dev server. Fix: use a local temp clone, clean validation artifacts, and stop only repo-owned processes; do not kill arbitrary listeners. [Task 1][Task 2]\n216-- Symptom: E2E accessibility or locator failures. Fix: make scrollable regions focusable and scope locators to the accessible location/component. [Task 2]\n217-\n--\n252-\n253-- rollout_summaries/2026-07-15T13-14-17-G1fU-phase_10_provider_independent_authentication_foundation.md (cwd=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\KG_Nolato_Summer_2026_Globalized_Development, rollout_path=C:\\Users\\kgray\\.codex\\sessions\\2026\\07\\15\\rollout-2026-07-15T09-14-22-019f65e9-ef40-79f3-8e4e-7b6a1860793e.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-15T13:59:29+00:00, thread_id=019f65e9-ef40-79f3-8e4e-7b6a1860793e, provider-independent foundation)\n254:- rollout_summaries/2026-07-14T15-54-47-nOms-eoat_atlas_repository_consolidation_phase10_authentication.md (cwd=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\KG_Nolato_Summer_2026_Globalized_Development, rollout_path=C:\\Users\\kgray\\.codex\\sessions\\2026\\07\\14\\rollout-2026-07-14T11-54-52-019f6156-83f5-7ab0-a85f-fbd639961490.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-14T22:05:47+00:00, thread_id=019f6156-83f5-7ab0-a85f-fbd639961490, initial schema/API/UI validation)\n255-\n256-### keywords\n--\n278-\n279-- `GET /api/v1/settings` exposes active non-sensitive settings anonymously; `PUT /api/v1/settings/{key}` rechecks live `settings.edit` and returns 401/403/200 appropriately. Tokens stay only in desktop memory while server stores SHA-256 hashes. Provider outage/change/permission loss relocks Settings and discards unsaved changes; normal app access remains available. [Task 1]\n280:- SAML/LDAP adapters fail closed. Production remains `NO-GO` until IT chooses/configures a provider, staging/security validation, vulnerability review, and human UAT complete. Use `docs/security/PHASE_10_REMAINING_IT_ACTIONS.md` rather than guessing. [Task 1]\n281:- Configuration state is factory defaults (`factory_defaults.json`), separately persisted user defaults, and mutable current settings; use `settings_store.py` transactional persistence and `settings_page.py` UI. Focused Settings validation passed 52 tests with seven skips plus Ruff/compile. [Task 2]\n282-\n283-## Failures and how to do differently\n--\n339-## Reusable knowledge\n340-\n341:- `release_metadata.json` is authoritative; `app/atlas/version.json` is derived. Run `python scripts/bump_version.py patch|minor|major --operation-id <stable-id>` only after implementation/validation, then `python scripts/check_version_bump.py --base HEAD`. The lock/receipt system prevents reused versions across concurrent chats. [Task 1]\n342-- Migration 20260715_0006 adds `application_releases`/build IDs/provenance. Test writes require child-process `EOAT_API_WRITES_ENABLED=true` and `EOAT_DB_NAME=eoat_atlas_test`; reset only the allowlisted test DB with `scripts/database/reset_mysql_test_database.py --database eoat_atlas_test`. Schema wrappers take `-DatabaseName`, not `-Environment`. [Task 1]\n343-- Crossfade transition cleanup must synchronously detach outgoing snapshot widgets before `deleteLater()`. Search navigation clears accepted text/selection/popup but retains invalid input; internal Home route is `minimalist_home`. ## Subagents started

Three read-only lanes are checking independent prerequisites while I own all mutations, port 3000, the one permitted full validation run, Git integration, and publication.

| Lane | Scope | Deliverable |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | Phase 4 governing records and acceptance gaps | Exact completion checklist and required evidence/doc changes |
| R2 | npm/launcher/runtime-mirror path | Safe way to run the canonical toolchain and refresh port 3000 |
| R3 | Phase 4 test/source risk review | Likely focused blockers and the smallest preflight test set |

They may not edit, fetch, install, start/stop servers, run `npm run validate`, sync chats, commit, merge, or push. All three worker slots are occupied; I retain final accountability.

[],\r\n          next: body.playthrough.crew,\r\n          previousVersion: requestVersion.current - 1,\r\n          nextVersion: requestVersion.current,\r\n          getId: crewIdentity,\r\n          getVersion: crewVersion,\r\n        });\r\n        const nextVoyage = { ...body.playthrough, crew: [...diff.rows] };\r\n        if (previous) {\r\n          const changes: string[] = [];\r\n          if (previous.status !== nextVoyage.status)\r\n            changes.push(`Voyage status changed to ${nextVoyage.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \").toLocaleLowerCase()}.`);\r\n          for (const id of diff.addedIds) changes.push(`${id.split(\"\\u0000\")[0]} joined the waiting crew.`);\r\n          for (const id of diff.changedIds) changes.push(`${id.split(\"\\u0000\")[0]}'s readiness changed.`);\r\n          setReconciliation(changes);\r\n        }\r\n        if (diff.addedIds.length) {\r\n          setNewCrew(new Set(diff.addedIds));\r\n          if (crewTimer.current) clearTimeout(crewTimer.current);\r\n          crewTimer.current = setTimeout(() => setNewCrew(new Set()), 800);\r\n        }\r\n        voyageRef.current = nextVoyage;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Project Lanternwake Phase 3 release plan: Unfurl the Tale\r\n\r\nThis section adds the Phase 3 Player cinematic-integration gate. It does not rewrite the accepted Phase 1/2 evidence, count a `PENDING` result as a pass, or claim final Rive artwork. The exact evidence form is `Development_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_3_Validation_Report.md`; the 57-row semantic visual index is `Development_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_3_Visual_Checkpoint_Index.md`.\r\n\r\n### 25.1 Controlling denominators and validator\r\n\r\nPhase 3 owns 189 unique accepted requirements, 90 Codex plus 99 OA. The 152 physical Phase 3 matrix rows contain OA carriers and are not additive. The Player baseline is exactly 102 cases: 17 event types Ã— six starting sections, full mode, 1440Ã—900. At least 185 distinct M1â€“M5 cases are required by the mode matrix.\r\n\r\nRun the read-only Phase 3 ledger gate before browser mutation:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython scripts/validate_phase3_player_event_ledger.py `\r\n  --ledger Development_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_3_Player_Event_Coverage_Ledger.csv `\r\n  --no-write\r\n\r\npython -m unittest scripts.tests.test_validate_phase3_player_event_ledger\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe validator must report 301 rows, 17/17 event types, 6/6 sections, 102/102 baseline cases, 20 Journal-opening rows, and 10 PageFlip rows. The program reconciliation gate must still report 458 accepted requirements, 361 matrix rows, zero accepted unmapped requirements, and zero unresolved requirements. Neither validator substitutes for implementation/runtime evidence.\r\n\r\n### 25.2 Persistent host and 102-case baseline\r\n\r\nFor every event from every starting section, assert:\r\n\r\n1. the sole persistent `player-progression` host remains mounted;\r\n2. the exhaustive event policy selects the declared global scene and readable outcome;\r\n3. queue ordering is deterministic by authoritative source, sequence, priority, and stable request ID;\r\n4. live/reconnect work precedes replay, duplicate and stale IDs do not animate, and replay never delays authority;\r\n5. exactly one required visible global target is selected from the correct host;\r\n6. the starting section is not changed to obtain a cinematic target;\r\n7. an optional local enhancement runs only after global handoff when its already-mounted exact capability is ready;\r\n8. unrelated local sections and targets remain untouched;\r\n9. `finalStateRuntime` settles or produces the declared readable fallback before cleanup;\r\n10. section scroll and the exact connected visible focus target are restored, with explicit destination/heading fallback;\r\n11. live/reconnect acknowledgment occurs only after an eligible global receipt; and\r\n12. all claims, handles, timers, listeners, focus state, audio, and runtime work clean up.\r\n\r\nComponent/unit evidence may enumerate this matrix, but the release gate also needs isolated Chromium mutation coverage and WebKit read-only presentation/accessibility coverage. A skip is individually classified and never added to the pass count.\r\n\r\n### 25.3 Replay, delivery, reconnect, and revocation\r\n\r\nUse the twelve-step replay protocol: automatic receipt; eligible completion; refresh; authorized bounded-history reconstruction; replay outside Journal; fresh request and scene identities; zero POST/PATCH/DELETE; no new business event; no new viewed row; unchanged snapshot/business state; focus/section restoration; and terminal cleanup. Replay always has `acknowledgmentEligible=false`.\r\n\r\nTest batch viewed-state filtering, duplicate delivery, query/subscribe overlap, reconnect gaps, source ordering, separate observed/queued/presented/acknowledged cursors, interrupted replay, failed mandatory retry, stale request rejection, and idempotent acknowledgment. On access revocation, retry and delivery terminate, protected workspace/history content is removed, and a readable access state receives focus. Snapshot sequence must never stand in for the presentation-acknowledgment cursor.\r\n\r\n### 25.4 Journal, PageFlip, and exact sections\r\n\r\nJournal coverage includes first/full, returning/abbreviated, completed/archive, manual-full replay, and reduced profiles. `JOURNAL_READY` requires a truthful receipt plus ready PageFlip or verified static fallback. Page turns assert all five lifecycle states, rebased queued page/generation, truthful same-page cancellation, current-visible-primary focus/target selection, stale generation rejection, and readable force-fallback behavior.\r\n\r\nSection-local checks use stable domain identities:\r\n\r\n- Journal: current-visible chapter/annotation identity, never hidden source or stale clone;\r\n- Chart: exact keyed location marker and route path/endpoints;\r\n- Treasures: exact artifact slot, silhouette, connection, dialog-local engraving, and focus return;\r\n- Quests: distinct exact quest note and objective identities;\r\n- Log: immutable progress-event ID row with separate ink/date/symbol children; and\r\n- Finale: exact requirement socket plus mechanism state/progress and nullable capability retraction.\r\n\r\n### 25.5 Modes, accessibility, viewports, and visual evidence\r\n\r\nRun at least 185 distinct M1â€“M5 cases: all 17 events in their relevant section plus chapter release, pause, resume, and revert from every section. Browser reduced motion is an upper safety bound. Mode changes may not recreate unrelated runtimes, replay one-shots, duplicate a presentation, or weaken content/focus/acknowledgment semantics.\r\n\r\nRun all six viewports â€” 2560Ã—1440, 1920Ã—1080, 1440Ã—900, 430Ã—932, 390Ã—844, and 844Ã—390 â€” with Chromium owning mutation and WebKit limited to read-only shared-database coverage. Assert zero task-caused serious/critical axe violations, one readable heading and controlled announcement, keyboard/touch reachability, correct modal focus, no hidden/inert/detached/stale focus, one accessible PageFlip content copy, no horizontal overflow/clipping, and readable behavior at 200% zoom where supported.\r\n\r\nCapture exactly 57 named semantic checkpoints: 11 Journal, 14 chapter, six map, seven artifact, five quest, five log, and nine finale. Every index row records label, event, case, section, mode, viewport, browser, artifact path, SHA-256, run ID, integrated SHA, status, and note. A missing file/hash/run/SHA remains `PENDING`.\r\n\r\n### 25.6 Production performance and twenty-cycle lifecycle\r\n\r\nThe performance gate runs only against the coordinator-owned optimized production server at `http://127.0.0.1:3200`, through `playwright.phase3-performance.config.ts`. Development FPS, unit fake clocks, and HTTP restart probes are not production performance evidence.\r\n\r\n| Metric                                    |        Phase 3 budget |\r\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------: |\r\n| Chapter release completion                | strictly `<10,000 ms` |\r\n| Target preflight p95                      |              `<50 ms` |\r\n| Skip/Replay/PageFlip response start       |             `<100 ms` |\r\n| Interruption/unmount cleanup              |             `<250 ms` |\r\n| Desktop frame-time p95                    |              `â‰¤25 ms` |\r\n| Mobile frame-time p95                     |              `â‰¤40 ms` |\r\n| App-attributable single stall             |             `â‰¤100 ms` |\r\n| Chapter cumulative long tasks             |             `â‰¤200 ms` |\r\n| Ordinary-transition cumulative long tasks |             `â‰¤100 ms` |\r\n| CLS                                       |               `â‰¤0.10` |\r\n\r\nAt least 20 cycles must exercise host mount/unmount, scene play/replay/cleanup, Journal/PageFlip open/turn/update/fallback/unmount, section enhancements, Quartermaster overlay, reconnect/revocation, audio, Lottie, and fallback transitions. After each group and cycle 20, hosts, targets, handles, generations, claims, runtimes, listeners, timers, EventSource instances, focus traps, clones, audio work, Lottie work, and pending WAAPI promises return to baseline with no monotonic growth.\r\n\r\n### 25.7 Serialized integrated gate and result classification\r\n\r\nOne coordinator owns the copied database, ports 3100/3200, browser, production server, complete Playwright run, production performance, build/restarts, Git, and final synchronization. The required order is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprogram reconciliation + Phase 3 ledger validators\r\n  -> focused policy/queue/host/Journal/PageFlip/section/API/security tests\r\n  -> format + lint + strict typecheck + complete unit/component + assets\r\n  -> prove unique copied-database identity through the running app\r\n  -> serialized Chromium mutation + WebKit read-only E2E, 102 cases, >=185 modes, six viewports, accessibility, 57 visual checkpoints, 20 cycles\r\n  -> optimized production build and owned production performance run\r\n  -> two restart/cleanup probes and canonical-database verification\r\n  -> one complete npm run validate result\r\n  -> Git/remote and one documentation/conversation synchronization proof\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse only `task-regression`, `pre-existing`, `environment`, `database-isolation`, `missing-phase-5-asset`, `blocked`, or `unresolved` for failures. The Journal Clasp, Voyage Compass, and Finale Mechanism final `.riv` binaries remain `missing-phase-5-asset`; their CSS/SVG readable fallback behavior is still a Phase 3 gate. No nonzero command, skip, missing artifact, blocked cell, or unverified total is a pass.\r\n\r\n<<<<<<< HEAD\r\n## 26. Project Lanternwake Phase 4 validation plan: Bring the Harbor Alive\r\n\r\n### 26.1 Denominator and deterministic tracking\r\n\r\nThe Phase 4 denominator is exactly 119 matrix requirements plus 122 OA requirements. `scripts/generate_phase4_manifest.py` rejects denominator drift, duplicate IDs, missing surface evidence mapping, or a stale 241-row manifest. Every row records source files, runtime, trigger, full/gentle/reduced behavior, fallback, test IDs, semantic checkpoints, implementation commit, and validation status. Final unmapped accepted requirements must equal zero.\r\n\r\n### 26.2 Focused surface gates\r\n\r\nRun each group once after its vertical slice stabilizes:\r\n\r\n1. platform tokens, one-shot identity, semantic polling deltas, authoritative async state, route/focus boundary;\r\n2. landing and Player/Captain/Creator authentication;\r\n3. invitation ceremony;\r\n4. Player Library, Captain Library, wizard, and waiting room;\r\n5. shell, notification, unseen acknowledgment, offline/reconnect and Ship's Log projection;\r\n6. Quartermaster preflight, receipt, reversal, reconciliation, conflict, and snapshot validation;\r\n7. Studio insertion, deletion/undo, drag ownership, validation, autosave, preview, publish, version, upload, comparison, and immutable states.\r\n\r\nUse the smallest failing test during repair. Filtered-out tests are not counted as passes or skips. Chromium owns mutations; WebKit remains read-only where the configured integrated suite permits it.\r\n\r\n### 26.3 Motion, accessibility, viewport, and lifecycle gates\r\n\r\nFull, gentle, system-reduced, product-reduced, and combined-reduced states must preserve text, focus, controls, authoritative order, and fallback. Required semantic checkpoints are listed in `Project_Lanternwake_Phase_4_Visual_Checkpoint_Index.md`. Automated axe scans cover idle, error, dialog, reduced, offline/reconnect, and created-result representatives. The configured six viewports must have no horizontal overflow or focus loss. After repeated mount/update/unmount cycles, hosts, targets, claims, listeners, timers, EventSource instances, focus traps, GSAP contexts, Motion presence, dnd-kit overlays, and pending requests return to baseline.\r\n\r\n### 26.4 Serialized integrated gate\r\n\r\nOne owner runs `npm run validate` once after focused gates and documentation stabilize. Its existing harness must prove the copied database path and nonce through the app, record owned PIDs/ports, run mutation cases only on the copy, verify the canonical SQLite family unchanged, run the full unit/component/browser/assets/build/lifecycle/performance sequence, and release resources. Any failure is classified before a focused repair; the full command is rerun only after the affected focused group passes.\r\n=======\r\n### 25.8 Final Phase 3 execution and acceptance record (2026-07-19)\r\n\r\nPhase 3 is complete under an explicitly approved composite-evidence decision. the continuation exposed the bounded retry, Journal readiness, PageFlip identity/settling, duplicate-host, and Library-exit failures |\r\n| Implementation repairs                 | **complete** â€” blocker fixes in `04b2114c91bad53bba0994aa54512d34dc740f5a`                                                                                                                                                                                                          |\r\n| Final targeted source gate             | **219/219 passed** with targeted TypeScript                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |\r\n| Final targeted browser gate            | **passed** â€” reduced retry/fallback and persisted acknowledgment, Journal target publication, PageFlip visible-primary resolution and settled behavior; ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T19:43:00.066Z

The cause is clear: an event received during reconciliation is discarded when React refreshes the SSE effect before the queued callback runs. I’m changing that guard so an already-received event continues unless the player view is genuinely revoked or unmounted; controller deduplication still prevents replay.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T19:43:04.699Z

Do not discard that authoritative delivery merely\n+      // because the old EventSource was closed; the controller deduplicates it.\n+      if (accessRevokedRef.current || !root.current) return;\n       if (event.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\") {\n         const next = await refreshSnapshot();\n         const authorized = next.presentationHistory?.find((candidate) => candidate.id === event.id);\n-        console.info(\"progression-debug:authorized\", event.id, Boolean(authorized), disposed);\n-        if (!authorized || disposed) return;\n+        if (!authorized || accessRevokedRef.current || !root.current) return;\n@@\n     source.addEventListener(\"progression\", (message) => {\n       const event = JSON.parse((message as MessageEvent).data) as ClientProgressEvent;\n       const gate = progressionReconciliation.current ?? const result = await tools.apply_patch(patch);

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T20:03:28.874Z

The browser timeout is a test race: the abbreviated ceremony completes naturally while Playwright is still waiting to click an already-visible Skip control. I’m making the test accept either legitimate completion or a successful skip; separate coverage already verifies the skip mutation itself.

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T20:32:17.033Z

page.locator(\".timeline-drop\").last();\r\n  await target.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded();\r\n  await source.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded();\r\n  const [sourceBox, targetBox] = await setInsertedId(id);\r\n  }\r\n  function openInspector(id: string, origin?: HTMLElement | null) {\r\n    if (origin) inspectorReturnFocus.current = origin;\r\n    inspectorFocusRequested.current = true;\r\n    setSelectedId(id);\r\n  }\r\n  function closeInspector() {\r\n    setSelectedId(null);\r\n    const frame = requestAnimationFrame(() => inspectorReturnFocus.current?.focus({ preventScroll: true }));\r\n    return () => cancelAnimationFrame(frame);\r\n  }\r\n  function focusBlock(id: string, origin?: HTMLElement | null) {\r\n    if (origin) inspectorReturnFocus.current = document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(`[data-block-id=\"${id}\"]`);\r\n      destination?.scrollIntoView({ block: \"center\" });\r\n      destination?.focus({ preventScroll: true });\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  function moveBlock(blockId: string, chapterIndex: number, blockIndex: number) {\r\n    change((next) => {\r\n      let found: Block | undefined;\r\n      for (const chapter of next.chapters) {\r\n        const index = chapter.blocks.findIndex((block) => block.id === blockId);\r\n        if (index >= 0) found = chapter.blocks.splice(index, 1)[0];\r\n      }\r\n      if (found)\r\n        next.chapters[chapterIndex].blocks.splice(\r\n          Math.min(blockIndex, next.chapters[chapterIndex].blocks.length),\r\n          0,\r\n          found,\r\n        );\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  function drop(event: React.DragEvent, chapterIndex: number, blockIndex: number) {\r\n    event.preventDefault();\r\n    try {\r\n      const payload = JSON.parse(event.dataTransfer.getData(\"application/x-tall-tale\")) as {\r\n        kind: string;\r\n        type?: string;\r\n        id?: string;\r\n      };\r\n      if (payload.kind === \"library\" && payload.type) addBlock(payload.type, chapterIndex, blockIndex);\r\n      if (payload.kind === \"block\" && payload.id) moveBlock(payload.id, chapterIndex, blockIndex);\r\n    } catch {}\r\n  }\r\n  function dndStart(event: DragStartEvent) {\r\n    const id = String(event.active.id);\r\n    setActiveDragId(id);\r\n    setDragAnnouncement(`Picked up ${id.startsWith(\"library:\") ? \"a new story block\" : \"story block\"}.`);\r\n  }\r\n  function dndCancel(_event: DragCancelEvent) {\r\n    setActiveDragId(null);\r\n    setDragAnnouncement(\"Move cancelled. The story order is unchanged.\");\r\n  }\r\n  function dndEnd(event: DragEndEvent) {\r\n    setActiveDragId(null);\r\n    if (!event.over || !draft) {\r\n      setDragAnnouncement(\"Move cancelled. The story order is unchanged.\");\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    const activeId = String(event.active.id);\r\n    const overId = String(event.over.id);\r\n    if (activeId === overId) {\r\n      setDragAnnouncement(\"Block returned to its original position.\");\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    let chapterIndex = -1;\r\n    let blockIndex = -1;\r\n    if (overId.startsWith(\"drop:\")) {\r\n      const [, chapter, block] = overId.split(\":\");\r\n      chapterIndex = Number(chapter);\r\n      blockIndex = Number(block);\r\n    } else {\r\n      chapterIndex = draft.chapters.findIndex((chapter) => chapter.blocks.some((block) => block.id === overId));\r\n      blockIndex = draft.chapters[chapterIndex]?.blocks.findIndex((block) => block.id === overId) ?? -1;\r\n    }\r\n    if (chapterIndex < 0 || blockIndex < 0) {\r\n      setDragAnnouncement(\"Move cancelled. A valid destination was not available.\");\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    if (activeId.startsWith(\"library:\")) addBlock(activeId.slice(\"library:\".length), chapterIndex, blockIndex);\r\n    else moveBlock(activeId, chapterIndex, blockIndex);\r\n    setDragAnnouncement(`Placed the block in chapter ${chapterIndex + 1}, position ${blockIndex + 1}.`);\r\n  }\r\n  function updateSelected(mutator: (block: Block) => void) {\r\n    if (!selectedId) return;\r\n    change((next) => {\r\n      const block = next.chapters.flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks).find((candidate) => candidate.id === selectedId);\r\n      if (block) mutator(block);\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  async function deleteSelectedAuthoritatively() {\r\n    if (!selected || !draft) return;\r\n    if (!window.confirm(`Delete â€œ${selected.block.title}â€? The block will remain visible if saving fails.`)) return;\r\n    const next = clone(draft);\r\n    const chapter = next.chapters.find((item) => item.id === selected.chapter.id);\r\n    const index = chapter?.blocks.findIndex((block) => block.id === selected.block.id) ?? -1;\r\n    if (!chapter || index < 0) return;\r\n    const [removed] = chapter.blocks.splice(index, 1);\r\n    setSaveState(\"Saving block deletionâ€¦\");\r\n    if (!(await save(next, false))) return;\r\n    setPast((items) => [...items.slice(-24), draft]);\r\n    setFuture([]);\r\n    setDraft(next);\r\n    setDirty(false);\r\n    setDeletedBlock({ chapterId: chapter.id, index, block: removed });\r\n    setSaveState(`Deleted ${removed.title} Â· Undo available`);\r\n    closeInspector();\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  async function restoreDeletedBlock() {\r\n    if (!deletedBlock || !draft) return;\r\n    const next = clone(draft);\r\n    const chapter = next.chapters.find((item) => item.id === deletedBlock.chapterId);\r\n    if (!chapter) return;\r\n    chapter.blocks.splice(Math.min(deletedBlock.index, chapter.blocks.length), 0, clone(deletedBlock.block));\r\n    setSaveState(\"Restoring deleted blockâ€¦\");\r\n    if (!(await save(next, false))) return;\r\n    setPast((items) => [...items.slice(-24), draft]);\r\n    setFuture([]);\r\n    setDraft(next);\r\n    setDirty(false);\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[]);\r\n    const visit = (value: unknown) => {\r\n      if (typeof value === \"string\" && assetIds.has(value)) ids.add(value);\r\n      else if (Array.isArray(value)) value.forEach(visit);\r\n      else if (value && typeof value === \"object\") Object.values(value).forEach(visit);\r\n    };\r\n    visit(draft.tale.coverAssetId);\r\n    draft.chapters.forEach((chapter) => {\r\n      visit(chapter.coverAssetId);\r\n      chapter.blocks.forEach((block) => visit(block.configuration));\r\n    });\r\n    return ids;\r\n  }, [data, draft]);\r\n  const assetContexts = useMemo(() => {\r\n    const contexts = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\r\n    const add = (id: string | null | undefined, context: string) => {\r\n      if (!id) return;\r\n      const values = contexts.get(id) ?? new Set<string>();\r\n      values.add(context);\r\n      contexts.set(id, values);\r\n    };\r\n    add(draft?.tale.coverAssetId, \"TALE\");\r\n    draft?.chapters.forEach((chapter) => {\r\n      add(chapter.coverAssetId, \"CHAPTER\");\r\n      const visit = (value: unknown) => {\r\n        if (typeof value === \"string\" && data?.assets.some((asset) => asset.id === value)) add(value, \"CHAPTER\");\r\n        else if (Array.isArray(value)) value.forEach(visit);\r\n        else if (value && typeof value === \"object\") Object.values(value).forEach(visit);\r\n      };\r\n      chapter.blocks.forEach((block) => visit(block.configuration));\r\n    });\r\n    data?.locations.forEach((item) => {\r\n      add(item.mapAssetId, \"LOCATION\");\r\n      add(item.displayAssetId, \"LOCATION\");\r\n    });\r\n    data?.artifacts.forEach((item) => {\r\n      add(item.artworkAssetId, \"ARTIFACT\");\r\n      add(item.revealVideoAssetId, \"ARTIFACT\");\r\n      add(item.modelAssetId, \"ARTIFACT\");\r\n    });\r\n    return contexts;\r\n  }, [data, draft]);\r\n  const recentAssetIds = useMemo(() => new Set(data?.assets.slice(0, 12).map((asset) => asset.id) ?? []), [data]);\r\n  const filteredAssets = useMemo(() => {\r\n    const query = assetSearch.toLocaleLowerCase();\r\n    return (\r\n      data?.assets\r\n        .filter((asset) =>\r\n          `${asset.displayName} ${asset.description ?? \"\"} ${asset.tags.join(\" \")} ${asset.roles.join(\" \")}`\r\n            .toLocaleLowerCase()\r\n            .includes(query),\r\n        )\r\n        .filter((asset) => assetMedia === \"ALL\" || asset.mediaType === assetMedia)\r\n        .filter((asset) => assetRole === \"ALL\" || asset.roles.includes(assetRole))\r\n        .filter((asset) => assetTag === \"ALL\" || asset.tags.includes(assetTag))\r\n        .filter(\r\n          (asset) =>\r\n            assetCollection === \"ALL\" || asset.collectionItems.some((item) => item.collectionId === assetCollection),\r\n        )\r\n        .filter(\r\n          (asset) =>\r\n            assetUsage === \"ALL\" ||\r\n            (assetUsage === \"USED\"\r\n              ? referencedIds.has(asset.id)\r\n              : assetUsage === \"UNUSED\"\r\n                ? !referencedIds.has(asset.id)\r\n                : recentAssetIds.has(asset.id)),\r\n        )\r\n        .filter((asset) => assetContext === \"ALL\" || assetContexts.get(asset.id)?.has(assetContext))\r\n        .sort((left, right) => new Date(right.updatedAt).getTime() - new Date(left.updatedAt).getTime()) ?? []\r\n    );\r\n  }, [\r\n    assetCollection,\r\n    assetContext,\r\n    assetContexts,\r\n    assetMedia,\r\n    assetRole,\r\n    assetSearch,\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

$env:PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL -ne \"http://127.0.0.1:3100\") {\r\n    throw \"Validation requires PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3100.\"\r\n}\r\nif ($env:FOREVER_VALIDATION_PRODUCTION_PORT -and [int]$env:FOREVER_VALIDATION_PRODUCTION_PORT -ne 3200) {\r\n    throw \"Production restart validation is serialized on port 3200.\"\r\n}\r\nif ($env:FOREVER_PHASE3_PERFORMANCE_BASE_URL -and $env:FOREVER_PHASE3_PERFORMANCE_BASE_URL -ne \"http://127.0.0.1:3200\") {\r\n    throw \"Phase 3 production performance requires http://127.0.0.1:3200.\"\r\n}\r\nif ($env:PHASE3_BASE_URL -and $env:PHASE3_BASE_URL -notin @(\"http://127.0.0.1:3100\", \"http://127.0.0.1:3200\")) {\r\n    throw \"Phase 3 validation base URL must use the harness-owned port 3100 or 3200.\"\r\n}\r\n$env:PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:3100\"\r\n$env:PHASE3_BASE_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:3100\"\r\n$env:FOREVER_PLAYWRIGHT_EXTERNAL_SERVER = \"1\"\r\n$env:FOREVER_PHASE3_PERFORMANCE_BASE_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:3200\"\r\n$productionPort = 3200\r\nif (-not $env:GM_USERNAME) { $env:GM_USERNAME = \"kato\" }\r\nif (-not $env:GM_PASSWORD) { $env:GM_PASSWORD = \"development-captain-only\" }\r\nif (-not $env:PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE) { $env:PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE = \"development-moonwake\" }\r\n$env:VALIDATION_ARTIFACTS = Join-Path $runtimeRoot \"artifacts\\validation\"\r\n\r\nfunction Invoke-IsolationHelper {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string[]]$Arguments)\r\n    $nodeArguments = @(\"node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs\", \"scripts/prepare-validation-isolation.ts\") + $Arguments\r\n    Push-Location $runtimeRoot\r\n    try {\r\n        $rawOutput = @(& $node @nodeArguments)\r\n        $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE\r\n    } finally {\r\n        Pop-Location\r\n    }\r\n    if ($exitCode -ne 0) {\r\n        throw \"Validation isolation helper failed with exit code $exitCode.\"\r\n    }\r\n    if ($rawOutput.Count -eq 0) { throw \"Validation isolation helper returned no result.\" }\r\n    return ($rawOutput[-1] | ConvertFrom-Json)\r\n}\r\n\r\n$seedDatabase = Assert-ValidationChildPath -Path (Join-Path $runtimeRoot \"prisma\\validation.db\") -Label \"Validation seed database\"\r\n$copyFileName = \"validation-isolated-{0}-{1}.db\" -f (Get-Date -Format \"yyyyMMdd-HHmmssfff\"), ([Guid]::NewGuid().ToString(\"N\"))\r\n$isolatedDatabase = Assert-ValidationChildPath -Path (Join-Path $runtimeRoot \"prisma\\$copyFileName\") -Label \"Isolated validation database\"\r\n$isolationReport = Assert-ValidationChildPath -Path (Join-Path $validationArtifacts \"database-isolation-report.json\") -Label \"Isolation report\"\r\nif (Test-Path -LiteralPath $isolatedDatabase) { throw \"Unique isolated validation database already exists.\" }\r\n\r\nfunction Invoke-ValidationStep {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name, [Parameter(Mandatory)][string[]]$Arguments)\r\n    Write-Host \"`n==> $Name\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n    Invoke-ForeverNode -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -Arguments $Arguments\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Assert-TcpPortAvailable {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$Port)\r\n    $listeners = @([System.Net.NetworkInformation.IPGlobalProperties]::GetIPGlobalProperties().GetActiveTcpListeners() | Where-Object { $_.Port -eq $Port })\r\n    if ($listeners.Count -gt 0) {\r\n        throw \"TCP port $Port already has a listener. Validation will not reuse or terminate it.\"\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Get-TcpPortOwnerIds {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$Port)\r\n    $connections = @(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort $Port -State Listen -ErrorAction Stop)\r\n    return @($connections | Select-Object -ExpandProperty OwningProcess -Unique)\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Get-ProcessIdentity {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$ProcessId)\r\n    $processInfo = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Process -Filter \"ProcessId = $ProcessId\" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\r\n    if (-not $processInfo) { return $null }\r\n    $creationTime = if ($processInfo.CreationDate -is [DateTime]) {\r\n        ([DateTime]$processInfo.CreationDate).ToUniversalTime()\r\n    } else {\r\n        try {\r\n            [System.Management.ManagementDateTimeConverter]::ToDateTime([string]$processInfo.CreationDate).ToUniversalTime()\r\n        } catch {\r\n            [DateTime]::Parse([string]$processInfo.CreationDate, [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture).ToUniversalTime()\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n    return [pscustomobject]@{\r\n        ProcessId = [int]$processInfo.ProcessId\r\n        ParentProcessId = [int]$processInfo.ParentProcessId\r\n        CreationTimeUtc = $creationTime.ToString(\"o\", [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)\r\n        CreationTimeUtcTicks = [long]$creationTime.Ticks\r\n        Depth = 0\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Test-ProcessIdentityMatches {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)]$ExpectedIdentity)\r\n    $currentIdentity = Get-ProcessIdentity -ProcessId ([int]$ExpectedIdentity.ProcessId)\r\n    return ($currentIdentity -and [long]$currentIdentity.CreationTimeUtcTicks -eq [long]$ExpectedIdentity.CreationTimeUtcTicks)\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Assert-ProcessIdentityMatches {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)]$ExpectedIdentity, [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Label)\r\n    if (-not (Test-ProcessIdentityMatches -ExpectedIdentity $ExpectedIdentity)) {\r\n        throw \"$Label process identity no longer matches its recorded PID and creation time.\"\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Test-ProcessDescendsFrom {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$ChildProcessId, [Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$AncestorProcessId)\r\n    $currentProcessId = $ChildProcessId\r\n    for ($depth = 0; $depth -lt 12; $depth++) {\r\n        if ($currentProcessId -eq $AncestorProcessId) { return $true }\r\n        $processInfo = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Process -Filter \"ProcessId = $currentProcessId\" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\r\n        if (-not $processInfo -or [int]$processInfo.ParentProcessId -le 0) { return $false }\r\n        $currentProcessId = [int]$processInfo.ParentProcessId\r\n    }\r\n    return $false\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Get-OwnedProcessTreeSnapshot {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)]$LauncherIdentity)\r\n    Assert-ProcessIdentityMatches -ExpectedIdentity $LauncherIdentity -Label \"Owned launcher\"\r\n    $allProcesses = @(Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Process -ErrorAction Stop)\r\n    $processesById = @{}\r\n    foreach ($processInfo in $allProcesses) {\r\n        $processesById[[int]$processInfo.ProcessId] = $processInfo\r\n    }\r\n    $depthById = @{}\r\n    $depthById[[int]$LauncherIdentity.ProcessId] = 0\r\n    for ($pass = 0; $pass -lt 24; $pass++) {\r\n        $added = $false\r\n        foreach ($processInfo in $allProcesses) {\r\n            $processId = [int]$processInfo.ProcessId\r\n            $parentProcessId = [int]$processInfo.ParentProcessId\r\n            if (-not $depthById.ContainsKey($processId) -and $depthById.ContainsKey($parentProcessId)) {\r\n                $depthById[$processId] = [int]$depthById[$parentProcessId] + 1\r\n                $added = $true\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        if (-not $added) { break }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    return @(\r\n        foreach ($processId in $depthById.Keys) {\r\n            $identity = Get-ProcessIdentity -ProcessId ([int]$processId)\r\n            if (-not $identity) { throw \"Owned process $processId exited while its identity was being recorded.\" }\r\n            $snapshotProcess = $processesById[[int]$processId]\r\n            if (-not $snapshotProcess -or [int]$identity.ParentProcessId -ne [int]$snapshotProcess.ParentProcessId) {\r\n                throw \"Owned process $processId changed identity or ancestry while its tree was being recorded.\"\r\n            }\r\n            if ([int]$processId -eq [int]$LauncherIdentity.ProcessId) {\r\n                Assert-ProcessIdentityMatches -ExpectedIdentity $LauncherIdentity -Label \"Owned launcher\"\r\n            } elseif (-not (Test-ProcessDescendsFrom -ChildProcessId ([int]$processId) -AncestorProcessId ([int]$LauncherIdentity.ProcessId))) {\r\n                throw \"Owned process $processId no longer descends from its recorded launcher.\"\r\n            }\r\n            $identity.Depth = [int]$depthById[$processId]\r\n            $identity\r\n        }\r\n    )\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Stop-OwnedProcessTree {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)]$ServerOwnership)\r\n    $recorded = @($ServerOwnership.ProcessIdentities)\r\n    if (Test-ProcessIdentityMatches -ExpectedIdentity $ServerOwnership.LauncherIdentity) {\r\n        $recorded += @(Get-OwnedProcessTreeSnapshot -LauncherIdentity $ServerOwnership.LauncherIdentity)\r\n    }\r\n\r\n{\r\n  \"name\": \"forever-treasure-companion\",\r\n  \"version\": \"0.2.0\",\r\n  \"private\": true,\r\n  \"packageManager\": \"npm@11.9.0\",\r\n  \"engines\": {\r\n    \"node\": \">=22.0.0\"\r\n  },\r\n  \"scripts\": {\r\n    \"dev\": \"next dev\",\r\n    \"build\": \"next build\",\r\n    \"start\": \"next start\",\r\n    \"lint\": \"eslint .\",\r\n    \"format:check\": \"prettier --check .\",\r\n    \"typecheck\": \"tsc --noEmit\",\r\n    \"test\": \"vitest run\",\r\n    \"test:watch\": \"vitest\",\r\n    \"test:e2e\": \"playwright test\",\r\n    \"assets:validate\": \"tsx scripts/validate-animation-assets.ts\",\r\n    \"validate\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/test-all.ps1\",\r\n    \"dev:full\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1\",\r\n    \"dev:stop\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/stop-dev.ps1\",\r\n    \"db:generate\": \"prisma generate --schema prisma/schema.sqlite.prisma\",\r\n    \"db:migrate\": \"prisma migrate dev --schema prisma/schema.sqlite.prisma\",\r\n    \"db:seed\": \"tsx prisma/seed.ts\",\r\n    \"db:preset\": \"tsx Hold an exclusive OS lock for the entire\n+    # run so another checkout cannot mirror into it mid-validation.\n+    $validationRuntimeLock = [System.IO.File]::Open(\n+        $validationLockPath,\n+        [System.IO.FileMode]::OpenOrCreate,\n+        [System.IO.FileAccess]::ReadWrite,\n+        [System.IO.FileShare]::None\n+    )\n+} catch [System.IO.IOException] {\n+    throw \"Another Forever Treasure validation run owns $validationLockPath. preseeded.prerequisiteEventId : null;\r\n        if (flow.eventType) {\r\n          expect(targetEventId, `${flow.eventType} needs an exact manifest replay identity.`).toBeTruthy();\r\n        }\r\n        const baseURL = test.info().project.use.baseURL as string;\r\n        await page.setViewportSize({ width: viewport.width, height: viewport.height });\r\n        await page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: \"no-preference\" });\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"fallback\") await installPageFlipReadinessFailure(page);\r\n        let replayEvent: ClientProgressEvent | null = null;\r\n        let replacementEvent: ClientProgressEvent | null = null;\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"interruption\") {\r\n          await installPhase3EvidenceProbe(page);\r\n          await installReadOnlyAuthoritativeEventSeam(page);\r\n          replayEvent = await readExactPreseededEvent(page, preseeded, baseURL);\r\n          const replacementFixture = readPreseededPhase3FixtureFromEnv(\"PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\");\r\n          const replacementSource = await readExactPreseededEvent(page, replacementFixture, baseURL);\r\n          replacementEvent = Object.freeze({ ...replacementSource, sequence: replayEvent.sequence + 1 });\r\n        }\r\n        await installPlayerCookie(page, playerAccessId, baseURL);\r\n        const unsafeRequests: UnsafeRequest[] = [];\r\n        await installReadOnlyNetwork(page, slug, targetEventId, unsafeRequests);\r\n        await installLiveRegionProbe(page);\r\n        await page.addInitScript(() => localStorage.setItem(\"forever-muted\", \"true\"));\r\n\r\n        await page.goto(`${path}?section=${flow.section}`, { waitUntil: \"domcontentloaded\" });\r\n        await openReadableJournal(page, slug, flow.kind === \"reentry\");\r\n\r\n        if (flow.eventType && targetEventId) {\r\n          await assertModalFocusAndRestoration(page, flow.eventType);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"replay\") {\r\n          const replay = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: /Replay (presentation|introduction)/ }).first();\r\n          await expect(replay).toBeVisible();\r\n          await replay.click();\r\n          const skip = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: /Reveal readable result|Skip ceremony/ }).first();\r\n          if (await skip.isVisible().catch(() => false)) await skip.click();\r\n        }\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"fallback\") await assertPageFlipReadinessFallback(page);\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"interruption\") {\r\n          expect(replayEvent).not.toBeNull();\r\n          expect(replacementEvent).not.toBeNull();\r\n          await assertAuthoritativeReplayInterruption(page, replayEvent!, replacementEvent!);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        await assertViewportAndAccessibility(page, flow.section);\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"reentry\") await assertReadableAtTwoHundredPercentZoom(page);\r\n        await expect(page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Sound off\" })).toBeVisible();\r\n        const announcements = await page.evaluate(\r\n          () =>\r\n            (\r\n              window as unknown as Window & {\r\n                __phase3Announcements: Array<{ politeness: string; text: string }>;\r\n              }\r\n            ).__phase3Announcements,\r\n        );\r\n        const progressionAnnouncements = announcements.filter(({ text }) =>\r\n          PHASE3_EVENT_CASES.some((item) =>\r\n            text.includes(policyForProgressionEvent(item.eventType).globalPresentation.heading),\r\n          ),\r\n        );\r\n        if (flow.eventType && targetEventId) {\r\n          const policy = policyForProgressionEvent(flow.eventType);\r\n          const matchingAnnouncements = progressionAnnouncements.filter(({ text }) =>\r\n            text.includes(policy.globalPresentation.heading),\r\n          );\r\n          expect(matchingAnnouncements).toHaveLength(1);\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

});\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function openReadableJournal(page: Page, slug: string, returning: boolean) {\r\n  if (returning) {\r\n    await page.evaluate((currentSlug) => sessionStorage.setItem(`forever-intro:${currentSlug}`, \"seen\"), slug);\r\n    await page.reload();\r\n  }\r\n  const open = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Open the journal\" });\r\n  await expect(open).toBeVisible();\r\n  await open.click();\r\n  const skip = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Skip ceremony\" });\r\n  if (await skip.isVisible().catch(() => false)) await skip.click();\r\n  await expect(page.getByRole(\"heading\", { name: \"The Voyage Journal\" })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 20_000 });\r\n  await expect(page.locator(\"[data-player-experience-root]\")).toHaveAttribute(\"data-journal-phase\", \"JOURNAL_READY\");\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function assertModalFocusAndRestoration(page: Page, eventType: Phase3EventType) {\r\n  const overlay = page.locator(\r\n    `[data-progression-overlay][data-progression-state=\"active\"][data-presentation-event=\"${eventType}\"]`,\r\n  );\r\n  await expect(overlay).toBeVisible({ timeout: 20_000 });\r\n  await expect(overlay).toHaveAttribute(\"aria-modal\", \"true\");\r\n  const requiredTargets = overlay.locator('[data-presentation-relevance=\"relevant\"]');\r\n  expect(await requiredTargets.count()).toBeGreaterThan(0);\r\n  await expect\r\n    .poll(() =>\r\n      requiredTargets.evaluateAll(\r\n        (targets) =>\r\n          Boolean(targets.length) &&\r\n          targets.every((target) => {\r\n            const style = getComputedStyle(target);\r\n            const bounds = target.getBoundingClientRect();\r\n            return (\r\n              style.display !== \"none\" && style.visibility !== \"hidden\" && Number(style.opacity) > 0 && bounds.width > 0\r\n            );\r\n          }),\r\n      ),\r\n    )\r\n    .toBe(true);\r\n  const stacking = await overlay.evaluate((node) => {\r\n    const content = document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(\"[data-progression-content]\");\r\n    return {\r\n      overlay: Number.parseInt(getComputedStyle(node).zIndex || \"0\", 10),\r\n      content: content ? Number.parseInt(getComputedStyle(content).zIndex || \"0\", 10) : 0,\r\n    };\r\n  });\r\n  expect(stacking.overlay).toBeGreaterThan(stacking.content);\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T22:26:37.503Z

Root cause found: the accessibility probe’s unthrottled mutation observer starves rendering when the full-motion page updates ARIA attributes. I’m making the probe schedule its scans on a timer, preserving its coverage without competing with the app’s render loop.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T22:26:41.294Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\US-VT-FS01\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\tests\\e2e\\phase3-accessibility-viewports.spec.ts\n@@\n   await page.addInitScript(() => {\n     const announcements: Array<{ politeness: string; text: string }> = [];\n     const seen = new Set<string>();\n+    let inspectionQueued = false;\n     const inspect = () => {\n@@\n       }\n     };\n+    const scheduleInspection = () => {\n+      if (inspectionQueued) return;\n+      inspectionQueued = true;\n+      // Full-motion scenes update ARIA attributes during their own render\n+      // loop. A task boundary lets that work settle before this test-only\n+      // observer scans the live regions again.\n+      window.setTimeout(() => {\n+        inspectionQueued = false;\n+        inspect();\n+      }, 0);\n+    };\n@@\n-      new MutationObserver(inspect).observe(document.documentElement, {\n+      new MutationObserver(scheduleInspection).observe(document.documentElement, {\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch); text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\US-VT-FS01\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\tests\\e2e\\phase3-accessibility-viewports.spec.ts\n@@\n     const announcements: Array<{ politeness: string; text: string }> = [];\n     const seen = new Set<string>();\n-    let inspectionQueued = false;\n     const inspect = () => {\n@@\n         announcements.push({ politeness, text });\n       }\n     };\n-    const scheduleInspection = () => {\n-      if (inspectionQueued) return;\n-      inspectionQueued = true;\n-      // Full-motion scenes update ARIA attributes during their own render\n-      // loop. A task boundary lets that work settle before this test-only\n-      // observer scans the live regions again.\n-      window.setTimeout(() => {\n-        inspectionQueued = false;\n-        inspect();\n-      }, 0);\n-    };\n     Object.defineProperty(window, \"__phase3Announcements\", { value: announcements, configurable: true });\n-    const observe = () => {\n-      if (!document.documentElement) {\n-        queueMicrotask(observe);\n-        return;\n-      }\n-      new MutationObserver(scheduleInspection).observe(document.documentElement, {\n-        subtree: true,\n-        childList: true,\n-        characterData: true,\n-      });\n-      inspect();\n-    };\n-    observe();\n+    // Scene-owned ARIA attributes can update at animation-frame frequency.\n+    // Sample live regions without observing that render stream directly.\n+    window.setInterval(inspect, 100);\n+    inspect();\n   });\n }\n*** End Patch";

{ targetKey: targetKeys[eventType] } : {}),\r\n    payload: value ? { value } : {},\r\n    confirmation: true,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function publishCommand(captain: APIRequestContext, fixture: Phase3CaseFixture, eventType: Phase3EventType) {\r\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n    where: { id: fixture.campaignId },\r\n    select: { currentSequence: true },\r\n  });\r\n  const response = await captain.post(\"/api/gm/commands\", {\r\n    data: commandInput(fixture, eventType, campaign.currentSequence),\r\n  });\r\n  const body = (await response.json().catch(() => null)) as\r\n    | (Phase3CommandReceipt & { error?: string; code?: string })\r\n    | null;\r\n  expect(response.status(), `GM command failed: ${JSON.stringify(body)}`).toBe(200);\r\n  expect(body).toMatchObject({\r\n    persistence: \"COMMITTED\",\r\n    publication: \"PROCESS_PUBLISHED\",\r\n    delivery: \"PUBLISHED\",\r\n    playerDelivery: \"UNCONFIRMED\",\r\n    playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\",\r\n    playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\",\r\n    event: { type: eventType, sequence: campaign.currentSequence + 1 },\r\n  });\r\n  return body!;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport const PHASE3_FIXTURE_CLEANUP_ORDER = [\"viewed-ceremonies\", \"campaign-cascade\", \"chapter-content\"] as const;\r\n\r\ntype Phase3FixtureCleanupIdentity = Readonly<{ campaignId: string; chapterContentId: string }>;\r\n\r\nasync function cleanupPhase3FixtureRecords(identity: Phase3FixtureCleanupIdentity) {\r\n  const failures: unknown[] = [];\r\n  const step = async (cleanup: () => Promise<unknown>) => {\r\n    try {\r\n      await cleanup();\r\n    } catch (error) {\r\n      failures.push(error);\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n  // ViewedCeremony has no Campaign relation, so it must be removed explicitly\r\n  // before the campaign cascade. ChapterContent is shared by relation rather\r\n  // than owned by Campaign and is released only after the campaign is gone.\r\n  await step(() => db.viewedCeremony.deleteMany({ where: { campaignId: identity.campaignId } }));\r\n  await step(() => db.campaign.deleteMany({ where: { id: identity.campaignId } }));\r\n  await step(() => db.chapterContent.deleteMany({ where: { id: Chart\",\r\n  treasures: \"Treasure Altar\",\r\n  quests: \"Side-Quest Ledger\",\r\n  log: \"Ship's Log\",\r\n  finale: \"The Final Seal\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nasync function installReadOnlyNetwork(\r\n  page: Page,\r\n  slug: string,\r\n  targetEventId: string | null,\r\n  unsafeRequests: UnsafeRequest[],\r\n) {\r\n  page.on(\"request\", (request) => {\r\n    const method = request.method().toUpperCase();\r\n    const pathname = new URL(request.url()).pathname;\r\n    if (pathname.startsWith(\"/api/\") && method !== \"GET\" && method !== \"HEAD\") {\r\n      unsafeRequests.push({ method, pathname });\r\n    }\r\n  });\r\n  await page.route(\"**/api/**\", async (route) => {\r\n    const request = route.request();\r\n    const method = request.method().toUpperCase();\r\n    const pathname = new URL(request.url()).pathname;\r\n    const locallyIntercepted =\r\n      method === \"POST\" && (pathname === `/api/player/${slug}/presence` || pathname === `/api/player/${slug}/viewed`);\r\n    if (method === \"GET\" || method === \"HEAD\" || locallyIntercepted) {\r\n      await route.fallback();\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    await route.abort(\"blockedbyclient\");\r\n  });\r\n  await page.route(`**/api/player/${slug}/events**`, (route) => route.abort(\"blockedbyclient\"));\r\n  await page.route(`**/api/player/${slug}/presence`, async (route) => {\r\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 204, body: \"\" });\r\n  });\r\n  await page.route(`**/api/player/${slug}/viewed**`, async (route) => {\r\n    const request = route.request();\r\n    const url = new URL(request.url());\r\n    if (request.method() === \"GET\") {\r\n      const requested = url.searchParams.getAll(\"eventIds\");\r\n      await route.fulfill({\r\n        status: 200,\r\n        contentType: \"application/json\",\r\n        body: JSON.stringify({ acknowledgedEventIds: requested.filter((id) => id !== targetEventId) }),\r\n      });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 200, contentType: \"application/json\", body: JSON.stringify({ ok: true }) });\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function installPlayerCookie(page: Page, playerAccessId: string, baseURL: string) {\r\n  await page.context().addCookies([\r\n    {\r\n      name: \"forever_player\",\r\n      value: playerAccessId,\r\n      url: baseURL,\r\n      httpOnly: true,\r\n      sameSite: \"Strict\",\r\n    },\r\n  ]);\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function readExactPreseededEvent(page: Page, fixture: Phase3CaseFixture, baseURL: string) {\r\n  await installPlayerCookie(page, fixture.playerAccessId, baseURL);\r\n  const response = await page.request.get(`/api/player/${fixture.slug}/snapshot`);\r\n  const body = await response.text();\r\n  expect(response.status(), body).toBe(200);\r\n  const snapshot = JSON.parse(body) as { presentationHistory?: ClientProgressEvent[] };\r\n  const event = snapshot.presentationHistory?.find((candidate) => candidate.id === fixture.prerequisiteEventId);\r\n  expect(event, `Read-only fixture ${fixture.caseId} must expose its exact presentation event.`).toBeTruthy();\r\n  return Object.freeze({ ...event!, payload: Object.freeze({ ...event!.payload }) });\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function installPageFlipReadinessFailure(page: Page) {\r\n  await page.addInitScript(\r\n    ({ eventName, failpointGlobal }) => {\r\n      const receipts: PageTurnLifecycleBrowserDetail[] = [];\r\n      Object.defineProperty(window, \"__phase3PageFlipFailureReceipts\", { value: receipts, configurable: true });\r\n      (window as unknown as Window & Record<string, string | undefined>)[failpointGlobal] = \"readiness-probe\";\r\n ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T22:38:19.726Z

The focused failure exposed a real Phase 4 issue: opening from a deep-linked section can omit the physical journal actors needed by the ceremony. I’m correcting the handoff so it opens on the journal, then restores the requested section before interactive progression begins.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T22:38:24.249Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\US-VT-FS01\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\src\\components\\player\\PlayerExperience.tsx\n@@\n   async function openJournal(forceFull = false) {\n     if (openingBusy.current || !root.current) return;\n     openingBusy.current = true;\n     setJournalOpeningNotice(null);\n+    const requestedView = viewRef.current;\n     const controller = new AbortController();\n@@\n       if (forceFull) {\n         flushSync(() => {\n           setResettingJournal(true);\n+          viewRef.current = \"journal\";\n           setView(\"journal\");\n           setOpeningPhase(\"ENTRY_IDLE\");\n@@\n       await advance(\"SEALED_PAGE_REVEAL\", \"seal-pressure\");\n       await advance(\"SEAL_BREAKING\", \"wax-crack\");\n       await advance(\"BOOK_SETTLING\", \"paper-flutter\");\n+      // The physical book is a required opening actor, even when a deep link\n+      // asks for another readable section. Restore that section only after the\n+      // book has settled, before progression can start against its local actors.\n+      if (!forceFull && requestedView !== \"journal\") {\n+        viewRef.current = requestedView;\n+        flushSync(() => setView(requestedView));\n+        await nextFrame();\n+      }\n       await advance(\"JOURNAL_READY\");\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

replacementFixture, baseURL);\r\n          replacementEvent = Object.freeze({ ...replacementSource, sequence: replayEvent.sequence + 1 });\r\n        }\r\n        await installPlayerCookie(page, streams diagnostics.\r\n        // The assertion below owns readiness through the player-visible control,\r\n        // rather than treating that transport lifecycle as a product contract.\r\n        await page.goto(`${path}?section=${flow.section}`, { waitUntil: \"commit\", timeout: 15_000 });\r\n        await openReadableJournal(page, slug, flow.kind === \"reentry\", flow.section);\r\n\r\n        if (flow.eventType && targetEventId) {\r\n          await assertModalFocusAndRestoration(page, flow.eventType);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        if (flow.kind === \"replay\") {\r\n          const replay = ),\r\n        );\r\n        if (flow.eventType && targetEventId) {\r\n          const policy = policyForProgressionEvent(flow.eventType);\r\n          const matchingAnnouncements = \"development-readiness-probe\");\r\n  await expect(book.locator(\"[data-pageflip-runtime], [data-pageflip-source]\")).toHaveCount(0);\r\n  const staticPage = book.locator(\".reduced-page-stage > [data-page-index]\");\r\n  await expect(staticPage).toHaveCount(1);\r\n  await expect(staticPage).toBeVisible();\r\n  await expect(staticPage).not.toHaveAttribute(\"aria-hidden\", \"true\");\r\n  const receipts = await page.evaluate(\r\n    () =>\r\n      (\r\n        window as unknown as Window & {\r\n          __phase3PageFlipFailureReceipts: PageTurnLifecycleBrowserDetail[];\r\n        }\r\n      ).__phase3PageFlipFailureReceipts,\r\n  );\r\n  expect(\r\n    receipts.filter(\r\n      (receipt) =>\r\n        receipt.phase === \"failed\" &&\r\n        receipt.reason === \"development-readiness-probe\" &&\r\n        receipt.fallbackStatus === \"fallback\",\r\n    ),\r\n  ).toHaveLength(1);\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function startExactReplay(page: Page, eventId: string) {\r\n  const notice = page.locator(`[data-progress-event-id=\"${eventId}\"]`);\r\n  if (await notice.isVisible().catch(() => false)) {\r\n    await notice.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Replay presentation\" }).click();\r\n    return;\r\n  }\r\n  const history = page.locator(\"[data-presentation-history] details\");\r\n  if (!(await history.getAttribute(\"open\"))) await history.locator(\"summary\").click();\r\n  await history.locator(`[data-replay-event-id=\"${eventId}\"]`).click();\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function assertAuthoritativeReplayInterruption(\r\n  page: Page,\r\n  replayEvent: ClientProgressEvent,\r\n  replacementEvent: ClientProgressEvent,\r\n) {\r\n  await expect\r\n    .poll(() =>\r\n      page.evaluate(\r\n        () =>\r\n          (\r\n            window as unknown as Window & {\r\n              __phase3ReadOnlyAuthoritativeEventSeam: { connectionCount: number };\r\n            }\r\n          ).__phase3ReadOnlyAuthoritativeEventSeam.connectionCount,\r\n      ),\r\n    )\r\n    .toBe(1);\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T22:44:04.520Z

contentKeys: readonly string[] },\n       signal?: AbortSignal,\n+      requestKey = viewedContentRequestKey(targetView, entry),\n     ) => {\n+      viewedContentRequests.current.add(requestKey);\n       setViewedFeedback({\n@@\n       } catch (cause) {\n+        viewedContentRequests.current.delete(requestKey);\n         if (signal?.aborted || (cause instanceof DOMException && cause.name === \"AbortError\")) {\n@@\n     const entry = entries[view];\n     if (!entry.contentKeys.length) return;\n+    const requestKey = viewedContentRequestKey(view, entry);\n+    if (viewedContentRequests.current.has(requestKey)) return;\n+    viewedContentRequests.current.add(requestKey);\n     const controller = new AbortController();\n-    const timer = window.setTimeout(() => void submitViewedAcknowledgment(view, entry, controller.signal), 0);\n+    const timer = window.setTimeout(\n+      () => void submitViewedAcknowledgment(view, entry, controller.signal, requestKey),\n+      0,\n+    );\n*** Update File: \\\\US-VT-FS01\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\tests\\e2e\\fixtures\\lanternwake-phase3.ts\n@@\n     let fixture: Phase3CaseFixture = Object.freeze({\n@@\n       preseeded: false,\n     });\n+    if (routePrerequisitesRevealed) {\n+      await db.viewedContent.createMany({\n+        data: [\n+          { playerAccessId: campaign.playerAccesses[0]!.id, contentType: \"map\", contentKey: mapLocationKey },\n+          { playerAccessId: campaign.playerAccesses[0]!.id, contentType: \"map\", contentKey: mapDestinationKey },\n+        ],\n+      });\n+    }\n     if (eventType === \"STATE_REVERTED\") {\n*** End Patch";

param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path, [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Label)\r\n    $resolvedPath = [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($Path)\r\n    if (-not $resolvedPath.StartsWith($runtimePrefix, **Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:15:38.788Z

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:19:00.327Z

The chunk cascade is gone; the focused WebKit case exposed the one real deterministic failure: its continuously animated “Skip ceremony” control never satisfies Playwright’s stability heuristic. I’m correcting that test interaction to use the visible control immediately, then rerunning that same single case.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:19:02.112Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\tests\\e2e\\phase3-accessibility-viewports.spec.ts\n@@\n   await open.click();\n   const skip = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Skip ceremony\" });\n-  if (await skip.isVisible().catch(() => false)) await skip.click();\n+  // The control remains intentionally animated while the ceremony is active;\n+  // force is appropriate once its visibility has established user reachability.\n+  if (await skip.isVisible().catch(() => false)) await skip.click({ force: true });\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:28:31.132Z

The one-frame delay alone is not enough in WebKit. I’m adding a post-teardown focus verification and one safe retry; it preserves the existing exact/trigger/heading policy and only acts if focus fell back to the document body.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:28:34.345Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\src\\components\\player\\PlayerExperience.tsx\n@@\n       // WebKit clears focus asynchronously when the active overlay is hidden.\n       // Restore only after that teardown frame so the destination retains focus.\n       await nextFrame();\n-      const restorationResult = restoreSection(restoration, sectionFocusTarget.current);\n+      let restorationResult = restoreSection(restoration, sectionFocusTarget.current);\n+      await nextFrame();\n+      const activeFocus = playerRoot.ownerDocument.activeElement;\n+      const focusWasLost =\n+        !(activeFocus instanceof HTMLElement) ||\n+        activeFocus === playerRoot.ownerDocument.body ||\n+        !isSafeFocusTarget(activeFocus);\n+      if (focusWasLost) restorationResult = restoreSection(restoration, sectionFocusTarget.current);\n       if (presentationRun.current === controller) presentationRun.current = null;\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

I found the architectural gap: the progression modal traps focus while active but had no owned return-focus behavior on close. I’m adding that at the modal boundary, with a safe section-heading fallback when the presentation was automatic.

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:32:55.148Z

The focused unit exposed the timing precisely: setting `inert` moves focus before a layout effect can capture it. I’m capturing the last valid content focus during normal focus events, before the modal activates, then returning to it after close.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:55:27.960Z

I found the real acknowledgement bug: the dedupe key was recorded before the zero-delay send, then a rerender could cancel the timer permanently. I’m making the key authoritative only once the request starts, while preserving single-flight behavior.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T12:55:32.003Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\phase4-formal-acceptance\\src\\components\\player\\PlayerExperience.tsx\n@@\n     const entry = entries[view];\n     if (!entry.contentKeys.length) return;\n     const requestKey = viewedContentRequestKey(view, entry);\n     if (viewedContentRequests.current.has(requestKey)) return;\n-    viewedContentRequests.current.add(requestKey);\n     const controller = new AbortController();\n-    const timer = window.setTimeout(\n-      () => void submitViewedAcknowledgment(view, entry, controller.signal, requestKey),\n-      0,\n-    );\n+    let started = false;\n+    const timer = window.setTimeout(() => {\n+      started = true;\n+      void submitViewedAcknowledgment(view, entry, controller.signal, requestKey);\n+    }, 0);\n     return () => {\n       window.clearTimeout(timer);\n-      controller.abort();\n+      // A dependency change before the timer fires leaves the request eligible\n+      // for the replacement effect. Once started, it is a single-flight write.\n+      if (!started) controller.abort();\n     };\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));
